title: Beat Window Loader
prompt:
Create a production-ready Beat Window Loader using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original deterministic loader represents a three-stage export check with three square windows. A person can select Prepare, Verify, or Deliver to preview each checkpoint; the selected window fills with a lime marker and a written status sentence changes below. The component is an embedded preview rather than an uncontrolled endless animation, so its state remains useful in compact cards and detail pages. Use a light Bento system with #F4F5F6 stage, white card, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, soft neutral shadow, #111111 primary text, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Lime is the sole bright accent and every surface must have a solid fill.

Use a labelled native button group with 44px minimum controls, aria-pressed, and an aria-live polite message. Click, Enter, and Space select each checkpoint. Move the marker with a spring at stiffness 310 and damping 24, and fade the text over 150ms ease-out. Do not use timers, intervals, random values, storage, remote assets, network requests, or browser globals. Keep all data local and deterministic, using transforms and opacity for motion.

At 320px, preserve the three labels and avoid clipped controls; at larger detail size, keep the loader pleasantly compact. Include visible 3px focus rings, logical order, AA contrast, and text that names the active checkpoint beyond the lime cue. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by snapping the marker while retaining only a brief opacity fade. Return complete client code, pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.
